title: Charge transfer processes
description: Predict how charge gets transferred by conduction between two objects.

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal cube with negative charge $-Q$ is brought into contact with an uncharged metal cylinder. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
The excess electrons on the cube are repelled from each other and some can move to the neutral cylinder.  
The correct answer is:

Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of charge.

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal cube with positive charge $+Q$ is brought into contact with an uncharged metal cylinder. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
The positively charged cube attracts some electrons to move away from the neutral cylinder.  
The correct answer is:

Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of charges.

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal cube with a positive charge $+Q_1$ is brought into contact with a cylinder of negative charge $-Q_2$, where $|Q_1| > |Q_2|$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
The positively charged cube attracts some electrons to move away from the negatively charged cylinder.
The correct answer is:

Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of charges.

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal cube with a positive charge $+Q_1$ is brought into contact with a cylinder of negative charge $-Q_2$, where $|Q_1| = |Q_2|$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
The positively charged cube attracts some electrons to move away from the negatively charged cylinder.
The correct answer is:

Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of charge.

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal cube and a metal cylinder are both uncharged and are brought into contact with each other. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
Since neither object has a net charge, electrons are not repelled or attracted to either object and there is no net transfer of charge.
The correct answer is:

There is no net movement of electrons.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of electrons.

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal cube and a metal cylinder both have the same negative charge $-Q$ and are brought into contact with each other. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
Since each object has the same negative charge, electrons are repelled equally by both objects and there is no net transfer of charge.
The correct answer is:

There is no net movement of electrons.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of electrons.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
Since each object has the same positive charge, electrons are repelled equally by both objects and there is no net transfer of charge.
The correct answer is:

There is no net movement of electrons.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of electrons.

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal cube and a metal cylinder both have the same negative charge $-2Q$ and are brought into contact with each other. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
Since each object has the same negative charge, electrons are repelled equally by both objects and there is no net transfer of charge.
The correct answer is:

There is no net movement of electrons.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of electrons.

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal cube with a positive charge $+Q_1$ is brought into contact with a cylinder of a positive charge $+Q_2$, where $|Q_1| < |Q_2|$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
Since the cylinder has more positive charge than the cube, electrons have a greater attraction for the cylinder and some electrons move to the cylinder from the cube.
The correct answer is:

Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of charges.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which choice best describes the net movement of charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Electrons are repulsed by negative charges and attracted to positive charges.
Since the cube has more negative charge than the cylinder, electrons have a greater repulsion from the cube and some electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
The correct answer is:

Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.

choices.content: Electrons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Protons move from the cube to the cylinder.
Electrons move from the cylinder to the cube.
Protons move from the cylinder to the cube.
There is no net movement of charges.
